title: Neon Honey Rush
prompt:
Glossy K-pop dance-pop at 124 BPM in F# minor — sidechained electro plucks, supersaw stabs, gated reverb snare, deep sub-bass, finger snaps, vocal chops, airy female lead with 3-part harmony stack and male ad-libs. Bright, flirty, neon-lit, anthemic.

[Intro] [0:00 - 0:12]
Filtered vocal chop loop, rising riser, soft 808 kick pulse, single piano stab.

[Verse 1] [0:12 - 0:32]
Midnight on my tongue, taste it like a spark / City got a heartbeat, beating in the dark / Lipgloss reflection, chrome inside the car / Tell me what you waiting for, baby, here we are

[Pre-Chorus / Drop Build] [0:32 - 0:44]
Filter sweep up, snare roll, vocal chops stutter, kick drops out last bar.
Three, two, one — geokjeong ma (걱정마) / Light it up, let it go

[Chorus / Drop] [0:44 - 1:04]
Four-on-the-floor kick, sidechained pluck hook, layered harmony stack, gated snare on 2 and 4.
Neon honey rush, sticky on my skin / Pour a little louder, let the night begin / Sugar on the dancefloor, melting where we spin / Neon honey, honey, baby pull me in

[Verse 2] [1:04 - 1:24]
Half-time drums, plucked guitar, breathy vocal, vocal chop responses.
Glitter on the pavement, hologram parade / Tangerine perfume and the promises we made / Run it like a record, spinning out the fade / Tell the moon to wait, we ain't going home today

[Chorus] [1:24 - 1:44]
Full arrangement returns, add tambourine, harmony stack widens.
Neon honey rush, sticky on my skin / Pour a little louder, let the night begin

[Dance Break / Bridge] [1:44 - 2:04]
Beat flips to half-time trap-pop, group chant, hand claps, rap-sung cadence.
Hey! Ppalli ppalli (빨리빨리) move it / Hot like a comet, prove it / Left, right, lose it / Honey on the beat, we choose it

[Final Chorus] [2:04 - 2:28]
Key lifts a semitone, full stack + male ad-libs ("yeah", "come on"), confetti synth.
Neon honey rush, sticky on my skin / Sweeter every second, do it all again

[Outro] [2:28 - 2:40]
Filtered chop fade, single snap, soft "honey, honey" whisper tail.

title: Neon Mile Marker
prompt:
Confident, driving retro-electronic synthwave at 104 BPM in F# minor; gated reverb drums, analog bass, lush JP-8 pads, chorused FM lead, and a lead male vocal with subtle tape saturation and 1/8-dotted delay. Slightly melancholic, cinematic, late-night highway energy.

[Intro] [00:00 - 00:18]
Arpeggiated saw synth, slow-build pad swell, distant tom fills, side-chained kick enters at bar 5. No vocals.

[Verse 1] [00:18 - 00:45]
Dry verb on vocal, muted palm-bass, soft hi-hats.
"Headlights cut the fog like a confession,
radio static humming through my chest.
I traced your name in the dashboard's reflection,
eighty miles from anywhere I'd rest."

[Chorus] [00:45 - 01:10]
Big gated snare, stacked vocal harmonies a 5th up, bright lead synth countermelody.
"Run me through the neon, mile after mile,
I'm a ghost in a jacket, learning how to smile.
Somewhere past the static, somewhere past the rain,
I'll forget your number, but I won't forget the name."

[Verse 2] [01:10 - 01:35]
Add shimmering bell synth, light arpeggio panning.
"Every exit sign is a promise I outran,
every diner window holds a younger man.
The moon's a cassette I keep rewinding slow,
playing back the places I was scared to go."

[Chorus] [01:35 - 02:00]
Full arrangement, doubled lead vocal, octave bass.
Repeat chorus lyrics with ad-lib "oh-oh" tag.

[Bridge] [02:00 - 02:18]
Drums drop to filtered loop, vocoder pad, half-time feel.
"Maybe love's just a frequency,
fading in, fading out of me."

[Outro] [02:18 - 02:40]
Full band returns, lead synth solo over chorus chords, long tape-delay tail, slow fade.

title: Midnight Ink Cipher
prompt:
Boom-bap hip-hop at 90 BPM in A minor, dusty sampled drum break with swung kicks and snappy snares, warm Rhodes chords, muted trumpet stabs, upright bass loop, vinyl crackle bed, confident male MC, sung female hook, smoky late-night mood.

[Intro] [00:00 - 00:12]
DJ-style vinyl scratch, crackle, a single Rhodes chord loop fading in, snare roll into the drop.

[Verse 1 - Rapped, 8 bars] [00:12 - 00:44]
Ink on the page like a map of the city,
Streetlights humming out a midnight committee,
Notebook pocket, every line a small witness,
Quiet on the corner, but the rhythm got business.
Vibes through the headphones, dusty and golden,
Every loop a letter from a story unspoken,
Footsteps tapping out a metronome motion,
Calm in the chaos, that's the cipher I'm holding.

[Hook - Sung, warm female vocal, layered harmony] [00:44 - 01:04]
Midnight ink, light on the page,
Drums in my chest, the city's a stage,
Slow it down, let the record spin,
Write it loud, let the quiet begin.

[Verse 2 - Rapped, 8 bars] [01:04 - 01:36]
Rhodes hum soft like a kettle in winter,
Trumpet whisper through a memory splinter,
Bassline walking down a hallway of neon,
Every step a thought I can lean on, lean on.
No flash, just craft, keep the focus narrow,
Straight as a string, true as an arrow,
Dust on the break, that's the soul of the matter,
Heartbeat steady while the cymbals scatter.

[Hook - Sung] [01:36 - 01:56]
Midnight ink, light on the page,
Drums in my chest, the city's a stage,
Slow it down, let the record spin,
Write it loud, let the quiet begin.

[Bridge - Instrumental break] [01:56 - 02:20]
Drums drop to filtered loop, vibraphone solo, vinyl crackle forward, bass walks under, brief scratch flourish.

[Outro] [02:20 - 02:40]
Rhodes chord sustains, crackle louder, drums fade, final scratch, needle lift.

Mix: low-pass on samples, tape saturation, side-chained bass, no modern trap elements.

title: Horizon Of Light
prompt:
Cinematic orchestral trailer cue, 90 BPM, D minor shifting to D major, full symphony orchestra with wordless choir, taiko ensemble, and solo female vocalise; mood is hushed-to-triumphant, hopeful and grand.

[Hush] [00:00 - 00:25]
Sparse low piano ostinato in D minor, distant sustained cellos, a single airy female vocalise floating above. Soft sub-drone enters at 0:15. Almost silent dynamic.

[Statement] [00:25 - 00:55]
Solo cello introduces the main theme over the piano ostinato. Light high strings shimmer in. Soft solo female voice sings the motif: "carry the light" — gentle, wordless ahs answer.

[Build] [00:55 - 01:35]
Add low brass swells, staccato strings layering in eighth notes, snare rolls building, taiko hits every two bars. Choir enters on warm "ooohs." Tension rises with rising string runs and cymbal swells; brief silence at 1:33.

[Climax] [01:35 - 02:10]
Full orchestra in bright D major, soaring violins on the main theme, heroic horns and trombones countermelody, pounding taikos and timpani, full mixed choir on triumphant "aaahs." Solo soprano returns singing "carry the light" twice over the climax.

[Aftermath] [02:10 - 02:35]
Sudden drop to solo piano and lone sustained violin, choir fades to a distant hum, last taiko echo.

[Reprise] [02:35 - 02:55]
Warm strings and soft horn restate the theme one final time, solo voice whispers "carry the light," gentle cymbal shimmer resolves on a sustained D major chord.

title: Lamplight Letters
prompt:
Intimate indie folk ballad, 72 BPM, key of G major, solo close-mic'd steel-string acoustic guitar with audible finger noise and fret slides, hushed breathy female lead vocal recorded close (4 inches), subtle room tone, soft brushed-snare entering late, warm tape saturation, late-evening lived-in mood.

[Intro] [0:00 - 0:18]
Fingerpicked G–Em–Cadd9–D, single guitar, room reverb, faint chair creak, no vocal.

[Verse 1] [0:18 - 0:50]
Close vocal, almost spoken, no doubles.
"Kettle humming, kitchen light low,
Your sweater hung where the back door goes.
I wrote your name on a foggy pane,
Watched it disappear, then wrote it again."

[Chorus] [0:50 - 1:18]
Vocal lifts gently, soft harmony a third up, guitar opens to strummed eighths.
"Oh, the lamplight knows,
Every quiet thing I never chose.
Stay 'til the records spin to static and snow,
Darling, don't go, darling, don't go."

[Verse 2] [1:18 - 1:48]
Add brushed snare, upright bass quarter notes, vocal still tender.
"Found your letter folded thin,
Underlined the part where you let me in.
Rain on the gutter keeps a slow drum time,
I hum your laugh like a borrowed line."

[Chorus] [1:48 - 2:10]
Fuller harmony, guitar still soft, no drum fills.
"Oh, the lamplight knows,
Every tender thing the morning won't show.
Stay 'til the candle is a thread of glow,
Darling, don't go, darling, don't go."

[Bridge] [2:10 - 2:28]
Guitar drops to single notes, vocal nearly whispered, breath audible.
"If love is a small thing kept,
I'll keep ours in the hours we slept."

[Outro] [2:28 - 2:55]
Return to fingerpicked G–Em–Cadd9–D, hum melody, fade with room tone and a soft guitar exhale. Restraint throughout; no big swells.

title: Velvet Hour
prompt:
Modern neo-soul / R&B slow jam at 82 BPM in F# minor, built on warm Rhodes electric piano, sub-heavy 808 bass, finger-snap percussion, brushed hi-hats, muted electric guitar licks, and a smoky breathy lead vocal with airy adlibs. Vinyl warmth, tape compression, sidechained reverb tails.

[Intro] [00:00 - 00:18]
(instrumental) Rhodes chord swells, distant rain, vinyl crackle, soft snap on the 2 and 4, hummed vocal pad fading in.

[Verse 1] [00:18 - 00:45]
Breathy lead, close-mic, light runs.
Lamp light leaning on the kitchen tile,
You pour the wine, you take your time, you smile.
Socks on hardwood, low hum from the vent,
My phone face down, the whole night already spent.

[Pre-Chorus] [00:45 - 01:00]
Layered harmonies, swell of sub bass.
Clock keeps slipping, I don't mind,
Slow is a language we both speak fine.

[Chorus] [01:00 - 01:20]
Full stack vocals, snaps wider, Rhodes opens up.
Stay in the velvet hour with me,
No phones, no plans, no place to be.
Sugar on your collarbone, shadows on the wall,
If the morning's gonna find us, let it find us small.

[Verse 2] [01:20 - 01:45]
Drier vocal, intimate.
Steam off the kettle writing on the glass,
You trace my name in cursive, watch it pass.
Cab outside is honking at the rain,
We pretend the world forgot our names.

[Chorus] [01:45 - 02:05]
Stay in the velvet hour with me,
No phones, no plans, no place to be.
Sugar on your collarbone, shadows on the wall,
If the morning's gonna find us, let it find us small.

[Bridge] [02:05 - 02:25]
Loose runs, falsetto, ad-libs.
(ooh) Don't move, don't move, (yeah)
(stay right there) breathe me in slow,
(mm) the record's skipping but I let it go.

[Outro] [02:25 - 02:50]
Rhodes solo fades, snaps thin out, hummed vocal tail, vinyl crackle closes.

title: Golden Hour Groove
prompt:
Afrobeats × amapiano fusion at 110 BPM in A minor, built around a deep tuned log-drum bassline, lush Rhodes and analog pad chord stabs, crisp shakers, woodblock accents, and a smooth lead vocal with stacked harmonies and melodic ad-libs. Warm, summery, romantic and danceable.

[Intro] [00:00 - 00:18]
Log-drum solo entering on beat 2 and 4-and, dry shakers panned wide, soft woodblock on the off-beats, distant pad swell.
(spoken, low) "eh... feel the evening, oya"

[Verse 1] [00:18 - 00:42]
Sun on your skin, low sky turning gold,
Bare feet on the rooftop, story still untold.
Mango on your lips, laughter in the air,
I been counting moments just to meet you there.

[Pre-Chorus] [00:42 - 00:56]
Hip swaying slow, neon starts to glow,
Say you'll stay with me until the morning show. Eh!

[Chorus] [00:56 - 01:18]
Dance with me late, golden hour groove,
Baby every heartbeat got me locked into you.
Log-drum talking, shakers in the breeze,
Love me like the summer, easy, easy please.

[Verse 2] [01:18 - 01:40]
City lights humming, palm leaves in the wind,
You're the sweetest chorus my heart has ever sinned.
Woodblock ticking like a slow heartbeat,
We move together where the rhythm meets the street.

[Chorus] [01:40 - 02:00]
Dance with me late, golden hour groove,
Baby every heartbeat got me locked into you. Yeah-yeah!

[Bridge / Dance Break] [02:00 - 02:20]
Log-drum solo, vocal call-and-response: "oya!" / "move it slow", "eh!" / "don't let go", airy adlibs floating over filtered keys, woodblock rolls.

[Final Chorus] [02:20 - 02:40]
Full stack harmonies, brighter pads, doubled shakers, ecstatic adlibs.

[Outro] [02:40 - 02:55]
Log-drum and shaker fade, whispered "golden hour... stay with me". Mix: warm low end, airy top, vocals slightly forward, tape-saturated bus.

title: Midnight Rain Frequencies
prompt:
Liquid drum & bass at 174 BPM in F minor, chopped amen-style breakbeat, rolling sub bass, lush Juno-style atmospheric pads, soaring ethereal female vocal, late-night introspective euphoria with cinematic uplift.

[Intro] [00:00 - 00:24]
Filtered amen break under deep evolving pads, distant vinyl crackle, soft Rhodes chords, sub swells, granular vocal flourishes time-stretched into airy textures.

[Verse / Vocal Build] [00:24 - 00:54]
Soft female vocal enters with reverb tail and subtle sidechain ducking; ghost snares emerge, ride pattern in 16ths, bass filter slowly opens.
Lyrics:
Rain writes static on the window pane
Neon bleeds across an empty lane
I hold the silence like a second skin
City hums the song I'm drowning in

[Drop 1] [00:54 - 01:24]
Rolling reece bass locks with sub on the offbeat, crisp chopped break, snare ghosts, shimmering pads sustain the lift.
Hook (recurs in Drop 2):
Carry me through the midnight frequencies
Every heartbeat is a memory
Headlights paint me gold and slow
I am the echo of letting go

[Mid-Section / Breakdown] [01:24 - 01:54]
Drums drop out, pads bloom wide, vocal stems pitched and granular, sub pulses every two bars, rising white-noise sweep.
Lyrics:
Somewhere a train is leaving without me
Somewhere the morning is learning to be
I trace your name in fogged-up chrome
The long way back is the long way home

[Drop 2] [01:54 - 02:30]
Full energy: layered breaks, reece bass widened, hook returns doubled with airy harmonies, ride drives 16ths, sidechained pads breathe.

[Outro] [02:30 - 02:54]
Drums filter away, sub fades, lone vocal tail and pad reverb dissolve into rain ambience and vinyl crackle.

title: Fracture Protocol
prompt:
Modern progressive metal / djent in drop F#, ~132 BPM with half-time grooves, 8-string guitar, double-bass kicks, cinematic strings, clean lead vocal with intermittent screamed accents.

[Intro] [00:00 - 00:18]
Clean ambient 8-string with volume swells, distant pad, soft cymbal wash, low sub drone enters.

[Verse 1] [00:18 - 00:42]
Palm-muted polymetric riff in 7/8, tight kick patterns, sung clean vocal, restrained.
Lyrics:
Under weight of every wire I built
Every promise stacked like bricks of guilt
I can hear the static rising slow
Somewhere underneath, a steady glow

[Pre-Chorus] [00:42 - 00:58]
Riff opens, toms build, strings swell, vocal climbs.
Lyrics:
Hold the line, hold the line
Until the silence breaks

[Chorus] [00:58 - 1:22]
Anthemic clean vocal with stacked harmonies, wide reverb cymbals, heavy chugged backing, soaring lead.
Lyrics:
Break me open, let the morning in
Every fracture is where light begins
I am louder than the noise within
I am louder than the noise within

[Verse 2] [1:22 - 1:46]
Groovier syncopated riff, half-time feel, scream tags on line ends (marked !).
Lyrics:
Count the cracks along the polished floor!
I was never built to take much more!
But the pressure carved a clearer name
Nothing left to lose, nothing left to blame

[Bridge] [1:46 - 2:08]
Atmospheric clean break, delayed arpeggios, soft pad, whispered vocal, strings rise.
Lyrics:
Quiet now, the storm remembers me
Quiet now, I finally see

[Final Chorus] [2:08 - 2:34]
Full dynamics, layered harmonies, double-kick gallop, cinematic strings peak, one shouted accent.
Lyrics:
Break me open, let the morning in
Every fracture is where light begins
This is where the new world WILL begin!

[Outro] [2:34 - 2:50]
Riff decays into clean swell, sub drone fades, single cymbal bell ring out.

Production: saturated yet defined 8-string tone, punchy click-forward kick, reverb-drenched cymbals on choruses, sub-bass reinforcement, stacked chorus harmonies, contrast loud-quiet-loud.

title: Petals In Static
prompt:
Dream pop / shoegaze track at 98 BPM in B major, ~2:50 long, awash in fuzz guitars, ethereal female lead buried behind the wall, rounded melodic bass, relaxed motorik drums; mood: drifting, weightless, bittersweet euphoria.

[Intro] [0:00 - 0:18]
Slow swelling guitar drone with volume-pedal bends, reverse plate reverb tail, distant tape hiss, single chiming chorus-soaked guitar arpeggio fading in.

[Verse 1] [0:18 - 0:46]
Bass enters round and melodic, soft brushed kit with relaxed swing, vocals enter low in the mix, smeared with long pre-delay reverb and an octave-up double.
Lyrics:
Light through the curtains slow
Paint on my eyelids glow
You were a color I forgot
Floating where the hours go

[Chorus] [0:46 - 1:14]
Massive wall of fuzz + chorus + tremolo guitars opens, three layered tracks panned wide, vocals push slightly forward with high harmony.
Lyrics:
Drift, drift, hold me under bloom
Sugar in the static room
Every summer I dissolve
Into the blue, into the blue

[Verse 2] [1:14 - 1:38]
Guitars pull back to shimmer, bass walks, vocals double-tracked whispering.
Lyrics:
Water on the window pane
Your name a soft refrain
Petals in a slower world
Breathing in the rain

[Chorus] [1:38 - 2:00]
Wall returns bigger, add reverse-reverb swells between phrases, harmonies stack a fifth above.

[Bridge] [2:00 - 2:18]
Drums drop out, only guitar drone + ebow + lone vocal floating.
Lyrics:
I was almost there
Almost air

[Final Chorus] [2:18 - 2:42]
Peak wall, snare crashes back in, feedback howls, vocals smeared, harmonies full.

[Outro] [2:42 - 2:55]
Long fade on feedback texture, decaying tremolo, last cymbal wash, tape stop into silence.

Mix smeared but musical, modern clean low end, no clipping.
